Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6690) ) has provided an announcement.

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. has scheduled a board meeting for 26 March 2026 in Qingdao to review and approve the audited results of the company and its subsidiaries for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. The board will also consider the proposal of a final dividend, signalling potential capital returns to shareholders subject to the company’s full-year performance and board approval.

The timing of the meeting indicates that Haier is on track with its regular financial reporting cycle to the Hong Kong market, which may provide clarity on its earnings trajectory amid global demand shifts in the smart home and appliance sector. Any decision on a final dividend will be closely watched by investors as an indicator of cash generation strength and management’s confidence in the company’s operational outlook.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6690) stock is a Buy with a HK$31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:6690 Stock Forecast page.

More about Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. Class H

Haier Smart Home Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated home appliances manufacturer listed in Hong Kong, focusing on smart household solutions under its flagship Haier brand. The company operates globally and offers a broad portfolio of connected refrigerators, washers, air conditioners, and other smart home products aimed at mid- to high-end consumers.

data.title: Wisdom Wealth Case Sent Back for HKEX Delisting Reconsideration as Trading Ban Persists
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wisdom Wealth Resources Investment Holding Group Limited ( (HK:0007) ) has shared an announcement.

Wisdom Wealth Resources Investment Holding Group Limited announced that the Listing Review Committee of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange has decided to remit its potential delisting case back to the Listing Committee for reconsideration, following a review hearing held on 20 January 2026. The move follows a series of delays in financial reporting, auditor changes, internal reviews and litigation disclosures that have placed the company under resumption guidance and regulatory scrutiny.

Despite the latest development, trading in the company’s shares remains suspended since 2 April 2024 and will continue until further notice, leaving shareholders and potential investors facing prolonged illiquidity and uncertainty. The company said it will issue further announcements in line with listing requirements, while advising investors to exercise caution and seek professional advice on the implications of any potential delisting or resumption outcome.

More about Wisdom Wealth Resources Investment Holding Group Limited

Wisdom Wealth Resources Investment Holding Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure that suggests a focus on investment holdings and related financial or resource-based activities. The company’s shares trade on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 7, and it is overseseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

Current Market Cap: HK$101.1M

data.title: SinoMab Ends Haikou Lease as It Shifts to Asset-Light Manufacturing Model
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SinoMab Bioscience Ltd. ( (HK:3681) ) just unveiled an announcement.

SinoMab BioScience has agreed to terminate a 20-year lease for its Haikou manufacturing facility with connected party Haikou Pharmaceutical, effective 12 March 2026, requiring its subsidiaries to vacate the premises within 15 days. The move follows prolonged negotiations and is framed as part of the company’s shift toward a light-asset manufacturing model.

Under this strategy, drug candidates previously produced at the Haikou site for pre-clinical and clinical use will be outsourced to contract development and manufacturing organizations, which the board expects will deliver significant cost advantages. The company says derecognition of the right-of-use asset under accounting rules will be treated as a disposal, but it does not anticipate any material adverse impact on its financial position or operations, positioning the change as beneficial for shareholders while constituting a connected transaction under listing regulations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3681) stock is a Sell with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SinoMab Bioscience Ltd. stock, see the HK:3681 Stock Forecast page.

More about SinoMab Bioscience Ltd.

SinoMab BioScience Limited is a Hong Kong–incorporated biopharmaceutical company listed on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The group focuses on the research, development, manufacturing and commercialization of therapeutics for immunological diseases, with a primary emphasis on monoclonal antibody-based biologics targeting global and Chinese markets.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

C.banner International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1028) ) has provided an update.

C.banner International Holdings has warned that it expects to swing to a net loss of about RMB150 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, compared with a profit of roughly RMB48 million a year earlier. The reversal is mainly driven by an impairment charge of around RMB149 million on trade debts owed by Mayflower (Nanjing) Enterprises and weaker revenue from its fashion footwear operations amid tariffs and subdued domestic consumer demand.

The company is still finalising its 2025 annual results, which have yet to be reviewed by its auditor or audit committee and may be subject to adjustments. Management has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care when trading the company’s securities ahead of the formal results announcement expected by the end of March 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CITIC Telecom International Holdings ( (HK:1883) ) has shared an announcement.

CITIC Telecom International Holdings has updated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, reinforcing a governance framework that emphasizes independent oversight and structured board renewal. The committee must consist of at least three directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and will be chaired by either the board chairman or an independent non-executive director.

The revised mandate empowers the Nomination Committee to set director nomination policies, maintain a board skills matrix, and drive diversity in board composition, including gender, background, and industry experience. It is tasked with assessing director independence, managing succession planning for key roles, reviewing directors’ time commitment and performance, and supporting regular board evaluations, underpinned by access to internal and external professional resources.

data.title: Sunshine 100 China Faces Winding-Up Petition Over US$205 Million Redemption Dispute
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Sunshine 100 China Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2608) ).

Sunshine 100 China Holdings has disclosed that a winding-up petition was filed against the company in the Hong Kong High Court on 6 March 2026 by HTI Financial Solutions and Haitong International Financial Products. The petition relates to an unpaid redemption amount of about US$205 million, and the company says it will seek legal advice and oppose the action, arguing it is not in the broader stakeholders’ interests.

The board has warned shareholders and potential investors that, under Hong Kong’s Companies Winding Up Ordinance, any transfer of shares or disposal of company property after the petition date could be void without a High Court validation order. The company also highlighted that Hong Kong Securities Clearing may suspend certain CCASS services for its shares, introducing additional trading uncertainty until the petition is dismissed, stayed, or a validation order is obtained, although no winding-up order has yet been granted.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2608) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sunshine 100 China Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:2608 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sunshine 100 China Holdings Ltd.

Sunshine 100 China Holdings Ltd is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong. The group operates in China’s property and related sectors, and its shares are cleared and settled through Hong Kong’s CCASS system, making it subject to Hong Kong corporate and securities laws governing listed issuers and shareholder transactions.

data.title: CITIC Telecom Posts Steady 2025 Results as It Deepens Macau Lead and Expands Regional Network
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CITIC Telecom International Holdings ( (HK:1883) ) has provided an announcement.

CITIC Telecom International reported 2025 revenue of HK$9,567 million, essentially flat year on year, while profit attributable to equity shareholders rose 1.1% to HK$920 million, with basic earnings per share up 1.2% and total dividends per share edging 1.1% higher to HK19.0 cents. Management highlighted stable overall operations and strengthening new growth drivers as it pursues its goal of becoming a leading digital and intelligent telecom enterprise in Asia Pacific.

Operationally, the group reinforced its market leadership in Macau, maintaining the top share in mobile with full 5G penetration, advancing 5.5G private networks, and upgrading fixed-line infrastructure with 50G-PON and Wi‑Fi 7 to move the city toward 10G connectivity. It also expanded its regional footprint by acquiring 100% of Hutchison Telephone (Macau), adding new and upgraded network PoPs in mainland China and Kazakhstan, boosting international capacity, rolling out digital and cybersecurity projects across Southeast Asia, and launching an ISP business in the Philippines, underscoring its push for broader international reach and technological leadership.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1883) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CITIC Telecom International Holdings stock, see the HK:1883 Stock Forecast page.

More about CITIC Telecom International Holdings

CITIC Telecom International Holdings is a Hong Kong‑incorporated telecommunications group focused on providing mobile, fixed-line broadband and international network services across Hong Kong, Macau, mainland China and the broader Asia-Pacific region. The company leverages its strong presence in Macau and alignment with initiatives such as the Belt and Road and the Guangdong–Hong Kong–Macao Greater Bay Area to position itself as a leading digitalised and intelligent telecoms provider.

data.title: Shanghai Bao Pharma Wins NMPA Nod for New KJ101 Gastroscopy Trial
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2659) ).

Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals has secured approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration to conduct clinical trials of its recombinant human chymotrypsin candidate KJ101 for dissolving and removing gastric mucus during gastroscopy procedures. The therapy is engineered via recombinant yeast fermentation to replace animal-derived chymotrypsin, offering advantages such as high purity, no animal-virus contamination risk, and reduced batch variability.

The company is also advancing KJ101 in Phase II clinical trials for several wound-healing indications, including burns, trauma, surgical incisions, pressure ulcers and diabetic foot ulcers, highlighting its strategy to build a broad, enzyme-based treatment platform. While management underscores the potential clinical and operational benefits of this synthetic biology product, it cautions that development, regulatory review and commercialization timelines for KJ101 remain uncertain and subject to typical clinical trial risks for investors.

More about Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co Ltd Class H

Shanghai Bao Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. is a China-based pharmaceutical company focused on developing biologic therapies, including recombinant enzymes, using synthetic biology and recombinant yeast fermentation technologies. Its flagship product candidate, KJ101, is a recombinant human chymotrypsin positioned as a safer, scalable replacement for traditional animal-derived chymotrypsin for use across multiple clinical specialties and wound-healing indications.

Average Trading Volume: 627,267

Current Market Cap: HK$16.05B

data.title: Swire Pacific Lifts Dividends as Underlying Profit Rises Despite Property Valuation Hit
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Swire Pacific ( (HK:0019) ).

Swire Pacific reported a 10% rise in revenue to HK$90.47 billion for 2025, with underlying profit attributable to shareholders up 9% to HK$11.37 billion and recurring underlying profit up 5%. However, reported profit fell 32% to HK$2.94 billion, largely due to a negative change in the fair value of investment properties amounting to HK$6.07 billion, which continued to weigh on headline earnings.

Operating profit increased 5% to HK$4.45 billion, and cash generated from operations surged 35% to HK$17.02 billion, helping swing net cash flow before financing from an outflow to an inflow of HK$13.48 billion. The board raised dividends per ‘A’ share by 13% to HK$3.80 as net debt fell 8% and the gearing ratio improved to 20.6%, signalling confidence in the group’s balance sheet strength despite softer return on equity and lower reported earnings.

Earnings per ‘A’ share on an underlying basis climbed 13%, even as basic reported earnings per ‘A’ share declined 29%, highlighting the divergence between core operating performance and property valuation movements. Equity attributable to shareholders per ‘A’ share rose 3% to HK$192.45, indicating gradual growth in book value and offering reassurance to long‑term investors focused on asset backing and dividend growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0019) stock is a Hold with a HK$77.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Swire Pacific stock, see the HK:0019 Stock Forecast page.

More about Swire Pacific

Swire Pacific Limited is a Hong Kong‑incorporated conglomerate with interests spanning property, aviation, beverages, and other trading and industrial activities. Listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock codes 00019 and 00087, it plays a significant role in the city’s corporate landscape and is closely watched by regional and international investors.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ruixin International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0724) ) has shared an announcement.

Ruixin International Holdings has outlined progress in meeting resumption conditions set by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange after a prolonged trading suspension, including the publication of all outstanding interim and annual results up to 2024, with the 2025 annual results expected by the end of March 2026. The company expects its auditor’s going-concern disclaimer to be lifted once it demonstrates sufficient funding and completion of its resumption plan, which hinges on capital injections from share and convertible bond subscriptions to restore a net asset position and support ongoing operations ahead of a March 2026 deadline for potential delisting.

Management believes that, following execution of the resumption plan and continued backing from its substantial shareholder, the group will have adequate financial resources to continue as a going concern and comply with the Exchange’s requirements. The Stock Exchange has yet to comment on the detailed resumption plan submitted in late February and early March 2026, leaving investors focused on whether Ruixin can complete the planned recapitalisation and governance steps in time to avoid cancellation of its listing after the 18‑month suspension period expires.

More about Ruixin International Holdings Ltd.

Ruixin International Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates as an investment holding company with interests that require ongoing compliance with the Hong Kong Listing Rules. The group relies on financial support from its substantial shareholder as it works to restore its financial position and maintain its listing status on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has shared an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has called a board meeting for 16 March 2026 to review operational performance, committee reports and the Company’s budget execution over the past 12 months. The board will also assess the General Director’s KPI achievements for 2024 and consider an internal corporate document as part of its oversight duties.

A key focus of the meeting will be the preliminary consideration and approval of RUSAL’s consolidated and separate 2025 financial statements under IFRS and Russian Accounting Standards, along with the related annual results announcement. Directors will additionally review the Company’s connected transactions for compliance with Hong Kong listing rules, underscoring ongoing attention to governance and regulatory alignment for investors across its listing venues.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company and one of the world’s major aluminium producers, listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges. The group focuses on the production and sale of aluminium and related products, serving industrial customers globally and reporting under both International Financial Reporting Standards and Russian Accounting Standards.

data.title: Hing Yip unit signs RMB320 million EPC deal for new Guangdong elderly facility
data.description:

China Investments Holdings Limited ( (HK:0132) ) has shared an announcement.

Hing Yip Holdings’ subsidiary Guangdong Taoyuan has awarded a major engineering, procurement and construction contract for a new elderly facility project in Guangdong Province to Guangdong Construction and Guangdong Qiyuan, following an open tender. The contract, valued at about RMB319.9 million, covers survey, design, procurement and construction, including specialized medical, HVAC, fire protection, and landscaping design, as well as post-completion technical and acceptance services.

Given that at least one of the transaction size ratios exceeds 100% under Hong Kong Listing Rules, the deal is classified as a very substantial acquisition and will require shareholder approval. The company plans to send a circular with further details and a notice of a special general meeting by early April 2026, signaling a significant expansion of its elderly care infrastructure portfolio and a potentially material impact on its asset base and future operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0132) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.21 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Investments Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0132 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Investments Holdings Limited

Hing Yip Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries including Guangdong Taoyuan in mainland China. The group is involved in development and operation of projects in Guangdong Province, with a growing focus on large-scale elderly care and related infrastructure facilities, positioning itself within the region’s social and healthcare services ecosystem.

data.title: 160 Health Expands “160AI Hospital” Ties with Public Hospitals in 2025
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

160 Health International Ltd. ( (HK:2656) ) has issued an update.

160 Health International Limited reported strong expansion of its “160AI Hospital” digital healthcare collaboration model in 2025, entering operational partnerships with 82 public hospitals of Class IIA or above by year-end. This represents a year-on-year increase of more than 720%, with 52 of those hospitals added in the fourth quarter alone, driving a 420% quarter-on-quarter jump in new collaborations.

The surge in hospital partnerships marks a significant step in market penetration for the group’s core digital healthcare business, extending its model deeper into the public healthcare system. However, the company stressed that these collaboration figures reflect only part of its operations and do not indicate overall 2025 financial performance, which remains subject to audit and potential adjustments, and it cautioned shareholders and investors to remain prudent when trading its shares.

More about 160 Health International Ltd.

160 Health International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the digital healthcare and wellness sector. The group focuses on technology-driven solutions for hospitals, with its core offering built around the “160AI Hospital” operational collaboration model aimed at public medical institutions.
